transcript.text:
[With 45 Years of Tradition?!]
[With many blog reviews]
It comes as fried rice
[Japchae Rice as fried rice...?]
[Signature Japchae (glass noodles) Fried Rice]
[Nanjawans (stir-fried pork meatball with vegetables)]
[Tangsuyuk (fried pork with sweet & sour sauce)] [Fried Dumplings]
[Jjamppong (spicy seafood noodles) & Jjajangmyun (black bean noodles)]
[Chinese cuisine party!]
[Quality you can see and taste...]
[You eat... a lot]
[It's so good that I can't stop!]
[Stop tempting me!]
Hello everyone
I'm here at Bomun-dong today
There's a restaurant known for their Japchae Rice

First time hearing about a restaurant known for Japchae Rice Their other dishes are highly praised too
And plus, they hold 45 years of tradition So I had to come to check it out
It's raining here What a perfect day to have some Chinese cuisine
Ta-da Here
[Andong Banjeom] [Seongbuk-gu, Bomun-dong]
Hello~!
Japchae Rice They're known for that dish
Tangsuyuk too
What should I get?
[A menu that will leave you indecisive]
What are some popular dishes here?
Our Tangsuyuk, Jjamppong, and Japchae Rice
How about platter dishes?
Kkanpunggi (spicy garlic fried chicken) And Nanjawans
Nanjawans? Should I try that one out?
How many years has this place been here?

From what I know, about 45 years
[The real owner shows up]
Well then...
1 jjajangmyun,
1 jjamppong,
1 japchae rice,
1 tangsuyuk,
1 nanjawans,
1 fried dumplings
[All of this...?]
I ran this place for 42 years
We used to be known for our Jjamppong
[She is confused that japchae rice is more popular than the jjamppong]
There are no places that make japchae fried rice like us
[I'll be your chef]
[Leave it up to me]

[When fried rice and japchae meets]
[Stir-fry the japchae too]
[Japchae (glass noodles) Rice complete]
[Nanjawans (stir-fried pork meatball with vegetables)]
[Jjajangmyun (black bean noodles)]
[Jjamppong (spicy seafood noodles)]
[Tangsuyuk (fried pork with sweet & sour sauce)]
Thank you
Thank you
Let's try one
[What..? Where did it go?]
Delicious
Did they fry the meatball?
It's something I never had before
[The rest of the dishes]
Thank you

Looks delicious
Jjajangmyun looks really good Let's mix this up first
Freshly made jjajangmyun This looks amazing
This is just what I like
Old-styled jjajangmyun
It's perfect Not too sweet
Wait, I think I'm going to end up finishing this I need to try other dishes too!
Tasty
Jjamppong
They're famous for the Japchae Rice But the owner says her jjamppong is the best
She said that this is good It does look amazing
Wow
Refreshing
Flavorful
It's not completely soup It has chili peppers floating on top
It has a clean taste

Tasty
Fried dumplings
It's chunky
With some soy sauce
This is great too
It's amazing with sweet & sour sauce too
[She would eat this every day if they delivered]
Tangsuyuk
A customer that was just here told me that the Tangsuyuk was incredible
It's good!
It didn't look too special to me
Looks like a traditional Tangsuyuk
It's not too hard,
Very crispy,
The insides just flake away...
I'm so bad with food expressions

It's delicious!
I'll finish this first before the noodles get bloated
Next!
Signature Japchae Rice
It comes as fried rice just like this
The portions are huge too
Get a good scoop
The glass noodles are long
So I'll eat the noodles first
It's very chewy!
Are these not glass noodles?
It is How is it this chewy?
Fried rice is tasty
Nanjawans
I had 1 earlier Its texture is very unique
How do I explain this?

[Let's see...]
Delicious
I love it
Mussels
Their business hours are very short
They close at 5pm
Open for 6 hours total
Close at 8pm on the weekends
Jjajangmyun is a masterpiece
[Focusing on the Tangsuyuk now]
[Is she going to be okay?]
Next
Japchae
Eating this separate is fine too The noodles are long and not cut short
Japchae on top
Are these supposed to be this chewy? That's so cool

I used to not have a preference on Dipping the sauce vs Pouring the sauce
But I'm leaning more towards pouring the sauce these days
Why am I only eating the Tangsuyuk?
It's so good
Stop!
Is the meatball deep-fried?
The meat is chewy too
Like sticky
Tastes good
Fried dumplings with sweet & sour sauce
Not much to it, but it's so good
Dipping in soy sauce is good too
I used to not eat it like this, but it's pretty good
Tasty
Lots of sauce
Completely soaked

When I was younger, my friends and I would argue over Dipping the sauce or Pouring the sauce
And ended up doing half and half Half soaked, half dipped
Nanjawans
Very similar to Tteokgalbi (grilled short rib patties)
Lovely
This should look good as a skewer
Nanjawans skewer
That must be a struggle to eat
Just eat what you like
I did order what I like
[Oh, is that how it is...?]
[After eating all the glass noodles...]
There's still a lot left
This should be good with jjajang sauce
This is just a fried rice
This is it!

Jjajang goes well with fried rice
Pay an extra 1,000 Won to have Japchae Fried Rice over regular Fried Rice
Last piece of Tangsuyuk that I've been saving
Drink some soup
[There's no such thing as being exhausted when it comes to eating good food]
[Cleared the Japchae Rice as well]
Last piece of fried dumplings
It's still so good
[Chinese cuisine party complete!]
Thanks everyone
-Did you enjoy the food? -Yes I did
-Thank you -No, thank you
-Does your stomach not hurt? -No, I'm fine
You should give the hospital a visit
Thank you! ♡ [Subtitles by Hyun Choi] [antharoni@gmail.com]
